events.text: Patient is a 55-year-old male of White/Caucasian race with an ECOG performance status of 1
At age 55, patient presented with generalized lymphadenopathy involving cervical, axillary, and inguinal regions with fatigue and drenching night sweats for one month
At age 55, excisional biopsy of a left axillary lymph node demonstrated diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, ICD-O site C77.9, histology code 9680/3

At age 55, pathology report showed large transformed cells with centroblastic features, Ki-67 of 70 percent, immunohistochemistry positive for CD20, CD19, CD10, BCL6, BCL2, negative for MUM1, consistent with germinal center B-cell (GCB) subtype by Hans algorithm
At age 55, flow cytometry confirmed monoclonal B-cell population positive for CD20, CD19, CD10, BCL6, negative for CD5, CD23, MUM1
At age 55, FISH analysis showed no MYC rearrangement, BCL2 rearrangement positive by t(14;18), no BCL6 rearrangement; single-hit BCL2, not classified as double-hit
At age 55, laboratory studies showed LDH elevated at 392 U/L, CBC with WBC 8.4, hemoglobin 12.1 g/dL, platelets 210, CMP with mildly elevated AST at 48 U/L, beta-2 microglobulin elevated at 4.2 mg/L

At age 55, staging PET/CT demonstrated FDG-avid cervical, bilateral axillary, mediastinal, retroperitoneal, and bilateral inguinal lymphadenopathy, largest axillary node 4.7 cm with SUVmax 19.8, no extranodal involvement
At age 55, bone marrow biopsy was negative for lymphoma with normal cellularity and trilineage hematopoiesis
At age 55, patient was staged as Ann Arbor Stage III with B symptoms (night sweats), IPI score 3 (high-intermediate risk) based on elevated LDH, Stage III, and B symptoms
At age 55, patient initiated cycle 1 of R-CHOP at standard doses with pegfilgrastim support starting day 2
At age 55, patient received cycle 2 of R-CHOP without complications, tolerating treatment well
At age 55, after cycle 2, patient developed grade 2 constipation and grade 1 peripheral neuropathy with mild paresthesias in feet, attributed to vincristine
At age 55, patient received cycle 3 of R-CHOP on schedule

At age 55, interim PET/CT after 3 cycles demonstrated marked reduction in lymphadenopathy, largest residual axillary node 1.4 cm with SUVmax 3.8, Deauville score 3, consistent with good partial response
At age 55, patient received cycles 4, 5, and 6 of R-CHOP, completing planned therapy without dose delays or modifications
At age 55, cumulative grade 2 neuropathy with numbness in bilateral feet persisted after completion of cycle 6, vincristine dose was reduced to 1 mg for cycles 5 and 6
At age 55, end-of-treatment PET/CT showed complete metabolic response, Deauville score 2, all previously noted lymphadenopathy resolved, consistent with complete remission
At age 55, patient achieved complete remission and was placed on surveillance with CT imaging every 3 months and clinical visits
At age 56, surveillance CT at 9 months showed no recurrence, LDH normalized at 188 U/L
At age 56, peripheral neuropathy improved to grade 1 at 12 months post-treatment, patient reports mild residual numbness in toes only
At age 57, surveillance CT at 2 years continued to show no evidence of recurrence

scenario_blurb: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL), germinal center and activated B-cell subtypes. Ann Arbor stage II-IV. Treated with R-CHOP. Include IPI scoring, PET-CT response assessment, some with relapse requiring R-ICE salvage and autologous stem cell transplant.
